patches.hypothesis: The method only adds the first value for a header with multiple values instead of combining them with a comma.
The method only uses the first value of headers when they should be combined with commas.
The method only processes the first value of headers with repeated names, instead of concatenating them with a comma.
patches.changes: - if (!values.isEmpty()) header(name, values.get(0)); + if (!values.isEmpty()) header(name, String.join(", ", values));
patches.diff: - if (!values.isEmpty()) header(name, values.get(0)); + if (!values.isEmpty()) header(name, String.join(", ", values));

patches.hypothesis: The method does not combine multiple header values of the same name into a single comma-separated string as required by the test.
patches.changes: Ensure header values are joined by commas before being set in the response headers.
patches.diff: Ensure header values are joined by commas before being set in the response headers.

patches.hypothesis: The issue lies in handling multiple headers with the same name. Currently, only the first header value is being set, but they need to be combined with commas according to the HTTP spec.
patches.changes: + Combine multiple header values with commas in the else block
patches.diff: + Combine multiple header values with commas in the else block
